'''Josh Walters''' (born 23 December 1994) is an English [[rugby union]], formerly [[rugby league]], footballer who plays as a [[Rugby union positions#Wing|Wing]] or [[Rugby union positions#Centre|Inside-Centre]] for [[Richmond F.C.]] in the [[National League 1]].

He played for the [[Leeds Rhinos]] in the [[Super League]], and spent time on loan from [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] at the [[Hunslet R.L.F.C.|Hunslet Hawks]] and [[Featherstone Rovers]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]]. Walters also spent a season with [[Featherstone Rovers|Featherstone]] in the second tier.

==Background==
Walters was born in [[Guildford]], [[Surrey, England]].

==Playing career==
===Leeds Rhinos===
A former [[rugby union]] player, Walters converted to league and joined Leeds Rhinos in 2013, making his Super League début in 2014 against [[Huddersfield Giants]].<ref>[http://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/rl-headlines/wait-over-for-leeds-rhinos-cross-code-forward-josh-walters-1-7269146 Wait over for Leeds Rhinos’ cross-code forward Josh Walters]</ref> He went on to make 8 appearances that year, scoring three tries. He made a further 9 appearances in 2015 and was also dual registered with [[Hunslet R.L.F.C.|Hunslet Hawks]].

Walters played from the bench in the [[2015 Super League Grand Final]], and scored the match-winning try in what was his first play-off appearance.<ref>[https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/general/rugby-league/super-league-grand-final-2015-match-report-josh-walters-secures-treble-for-leeds-rhinos-to-give-a6689426.html Super League Grand Final 2015 match report: Josh Walters secures treble for Leeds Rhinos to give legends perfect send-off]</ref>
[[File:Josh Walters.jpg|thumb|right|Walters playing for Featherstone on loan in 2016]]

===Featherstone Rovers===
Walters joined Featherstone ahead of the 2019 Championship season.

===London Broncos===
He moved to the capital following the Broncos relegation at the conclusion of the [[2019 Super League season]].

===West End Warriors===
Made two appearances for the West End Warriors in 2025, scoring the winning try during golden point winning the grand final.

==Honours==
[[Super League]]: 2015

[[League Leaders' Shield]]: 2015

[[Challenge Cup]]: 2015

[[SRL Mens Premiership]]: 2025
